commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=487490eab9b7842832fcf47352abc04e3d5095b2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the OpenAI Responses parameter builder and regression tests so prior reasoning, assistant text, and function-call output item IDs are omitted from replay payloads when building store-disabled Responses requests.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main builds Responses requests with `storeMode: "disable"` while still replaying prior item IDs for non-native-Codex Responses paths. I did not run a live provider reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: This needs contributor real behavior proof and a maintainer review of the store-policy replay fix before merge; automation should not repair around the missing real setup proof.

target.title: fix: avoid replaying Responses item ids when store is disabled
target.body:
## Summary

- Avoid replaying persisted OpenAI Responses item ids when the outgoing request disables `store`.
- Keep reasoning/text/tool-call replay metadata usable without sending stale server-owned item ids.
- Update Responses transport regression tests for `store: false`, including a `mycodex`/OpenAI Responses case.

## Why

When OpenClaw sends OpenAI Responses requests with `store: false`, server-side item ids from a previous turn are not persisted by the provider. Replaying those ids in the next turn can make the provider reject the request with:

```text
Item with id '...' not found. Items are not persisted when store is set to false.
```

This was observed after a long Codex/OpenAI Responses agent turn where the follow-up turn replayed prior reasoning/text/function-call item ids.

## Testing

- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:test:src`
target.author: zhanghang02
